item.title: Fed's Cook says the balance of risks has shifted toward inflation due to Iran war
item.description: Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook said ​on Thursday the war in Iran has shifted the balance of risks ‌for the central bank's dual mandate of price stability and full employment more toward inflation.

item.title: Netflix raises subscription prices across all plans in US
item.description: Netflix has increased prices on all its plans in the U.S., as the streaming giant ​pushes into new programming formats such as ‌video podcasts and live sporting events.

item.title: Unilever sued for defamation by ousted chair of Ben & Jerry's board
item.description: Unilever and its recently spun ​off Magnum ice ‌cream unit were sued for defamation ​on Thursday by ​Anuradha Mittal, who was ⁠ousted in ​December as chair ​of Ben & Jerry's independent board.

item.title: Trading Day: Sell everything (except oil)
item.description: The price of stocks, bonds and gold fell sharply on Thursday while oil surged, as fading hopes for de-escalation in the Middle East reignited inflation fears and left investors approaching the quarter end in a deeply gloomy mood.

item.title: Instant View: Nasdaq confirms correction as U.S. stock retreat gathers steam
item.description: The Nasdaq Composite index on Thursday confirmed a correction denoting a decline of 10% or more from its October 29 record high, the latest setback for U.S. markets approaching one month of the war on Iran.

item.title: JPMorgan files for private credit fund that allows 7.5% redemptions
item.description: JPMorgan Chase has filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission for a private ​credit fund that will allow investors ‌to redeem 7.5% quarterly, as withdrawal pressures build across the industry.

item.title: Nasdaq Composite confirms correction as war worries weigh
item.description: The Nasdaq Composite dropped 2.4% on Thursday, leaving the tech-heavy index down nearly 11% ​from its record-high close on October 29 and ‌confirming it has been in a correction since then.

item.title: EU reaches deal to fine online platforms importing products deemed unsafe
item.description: The European Union has reached an informal agreement to fine ​online platforms importing unsafe products, in ‌a move which it said was aimed at tackling cheap and possibly unsafe Chinese goods ​entering the EU via platforms ​such as Temu and Shein.

item.title: New Mexico lays out options to force changes at Meta after court win
item.description: Talk about regulating social media to make it safer for children often gravitates around the U.S. Capitol and the EU headquarters in Brussels. But following a New Mexico jury verdict, a district court in Santa Fe carries some heft, too.

item.title: Stressed US grid forcing data centers to get more flexible
item.description: The U.S. technology industry is being pushed to shrink its power use in times of high demand, amid growing public concern ​that Big Tech's massive electricity needs for its expansion of data centers are maxing out the country's grid.

item.title: Exclusive: Constellation exec says grid operator told company Three Mile Island can't connect until 2031
item.description: U.S. grid operator PJM ​has told Constellation Energy that the former Three Mile ‌Island nuclear power plant in Pennsylvania likely will not be able to connect to the grid until 2031, four years later than planned, the company said ​at the CERAWeek energy conference in Houston on Thursday.

item.title: Big Oil to reap billions from Iran war windfall after a month of soaring energy prices
item.description: As Big Oil executives gathered this week and discussed the biggest-ever disruption to global energy supplies due to the war in Iran, there was one impact they did not address publicly: the multibillion-dollar windfall they will make because of soaring prices for the energy they sell.

item.title: Meta boosts Texas AI data center investment to $10 billion
item.description: Meta said on Thursday it is increasing ​investment in its El Paso, Texas AI data center ‌to $10 billion, a more than sixfold jump, as it aims to hit 1-gigawatt capacity ahead of the facility's projected opening in 2028.

item.title: French education ministry reports TikTok to Paris prosecutor
item.description: Social media app TikTok has ​been reported to the ‌Paris prosecutor's department by the French education ​ministry over its ​possible links to mental health ⁠problems for young ​people due to excessive ​time spent in front of screens, the education ministry ​said on Thursday.

item.title: Food safety nonprofit's lawsuit claims Panera Bread misled consumers, citing Reuters
item.description: A food safety and animal welfare nonprofit filed suit against Panera Bread last week ​based on Reuters reporting, claiming the company misled ‌consumers about Panera’s meat products.

item.title: Exclusive: Occidental's Hollub, US oil's most powerful woman, prepares to hand over reins
item.description: Vicki Hollub's exit would mark an end to more than four decades at the Houston-based oil producer, where she became the first woman to become the CEO of a major oil company.

item.title: Mastercard explores sale of payments unit it bought from Nets in 2019, FT reports
item.description: Mastercard is seeking to sell the real-time payments unit that it acquired from Denmark's ​Nets Group in 2019 for $3.2 billion, the Financial ‌Times reported on Thursday, citing people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Apple plans to open Siri to rival AI services, Bloomberg News reports
item.description: Apple plans to open its Siri voice assistant to rival artificial intelligence services beyond its current ​partnership with ChatGPT, Bloomberg News reported on ‌Thursday, citing people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Exclusive: Musk rewrites IPO playbook with large slice of SpaceX stock for retail investors, source says
item.description: Elon Musk is discussing allocating as much as 30% of SpaceX’s initial public offering to individual investors — at least three ​times the usual retail slice — leaning on his rabid fan base and other loyal backers to help steady the stock after its debut, a person ‌familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Trump officials tout US energy dominance as global oil execs warn of supply crisis
item.description: U.S. officials this week said the historic spike in fuel prices during the war in Iran will be short-term and trumpeted record U.S. production at an industry conference where oil executives and government officials ​from Asia, the Middle East and Europe bemoaned the worst oil and gas supply disruption in decades.

item.title: CERAWEEK Private firms spearhead global shale push as US public operators stay home
item.description: Private energy companies and their backers are emerging as leaders ​in the next phase of global shale development, taking early positions in overseas basins while publicly listed U.S. producers focus on capital discipline and their core ‌domestic acreage.

item.title: Microsoft freezes hiring in major cloud, sales groups, The Information reports
item.description: Microsoft executives ​have in recent ‌weeks told managers ​at ​major divisions, including ⁠its ​cloud unit ​and North American sales groups, ​to ​suspend new hiring, ‌The ⁠Information reported on Thursday, citing ​three ​employees ⁠with direct ​knowledge of ​the ⁠decision.

item.title: Musk urges judge to review Twitter verdict, accuses jury of 'mocking' him
item.description: A lawyer ​for Elon Musk urged a federal judge to review a recent jury verdict finding that Musk ‌defrauded Twitter investors when buying the social media company, saying jurors improperly used the verdict to "send a message" by finding him liable.

item.title: Bank of Canada says it will have a tough job tackling structural changes
item.description: By Promit Mukherjee and David Ljunggren

item.title: United Airlines reaches tentative labor deal with flight attendants
item.description: United ​Airlines flight ‌attendants have reached ​a ​new tentative labor ⁠agreement ​with the ​company, their union said ​on ​Thursday.

item.title: Crypto platform Anchorage brings Sun's Tron to US investors
item.description: Anchorage Digital, a federally regulated U.S. crypto platform, said on Thursday it would add Justin Sun's Tron ​blockchain to its network, expanding the venture's access to U.S. ‌investors.

item.title: Stryker says manufacturing mostly restored after cyberattack
item.description: Medical device maker Stryker said on Thursday its operations were steadily improving towards full ​capacity, after a cyberattack caused widespread ‌disruption to its business.

item.title: Pernod Ricard has held merger talks with Jack Daniel's maker, source says
item.description: France's Pernod Ricard and Jack Daniel's owner Brown‑Forman have held talks about a possible merger, a source familiar with the ​matter told Reuters, a move that would unite the world's second-largest spirits maker with the largest ‌player in American whiskey.

item.title: Meta shares slip after US jury verdicts raise concerns of new legal exposure
item.description: Meta shares fell 6% to a 10-month low on Thursday after rulings this week found the Facebook parent failed to adequately warn or ​protect young users, stoking concerns about billions of dollars in fines ‌from new cases and follow-on litigation.

item.title: Peso mexicano se negocia estable atento a Medio Oriente, aguarda decisión Banxico
item.description: El peso mexicano mostraba pocos cambios el jueves, mientras que la bolsa caía en un mercado sin rumbo debido a la cautela de los ​inversionistas atentos a noticias sobre la posibilidad de un pronto fin del conflicto ‌en Medio Oriente.
